name: Cheese Fondue, American Style
recipeIngredient: 5 Fresh garlic cloves (or 1 tbsp. garlic powder)
French Bread (baguettes if available; heat in oven at 350°F for about 10 minutes, then cut into bite-sized pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the cheese soup in a fondue pot or heavy skillet over low heat.
2. Slowly add grated cheddar cheese, Kirsch, and garlic (for best results, use freshly pressed). If the mixture looks too thin, gradually add 2-3 tbsp of flour mixed with a small amount of cold milk until smooth, then slowly incorporate it into the pot while stirring.
DIPPERS:
FRENCH BREAD: Heat baguettes in the oven at 350°F for about 10 minutes, then cut into bite-sized pieces. Ideal when crusty.
MEAT DIPPERS: Cut ham, shrimp, smoked links, cocktail wieners, smoked sausage, turkey breast, chicken breast, hot dogs into bite-sized pieces.
VEGETABLE DIPPERS: Slightly cook (but do not overcook) broccoli, cauliflower, carrots, mushrooms, Brussels sprouts, cherry tomatoes, celery stalks, and radishes. They should be easily balanced on the fondue fork.
VARIATION: Substitute Campbell's Nacho Cheese Soup for cheddar cheese soup, or add finely chopped jalapeño peppers to the cheese mixture for extra zest.
Serve in a fondue pot with individual fondue forks placed in bowls of dippers. Guests spear a dapper, dip it into the cheese sauce, twirl it slowly, and balance it into their mouths. Enjoy!
